Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The paper explicitly states 'Hereafter, we referred to AT1G03106 as Sun Wu-Kong (SWK)'.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: SWK is described as a de novo gene/protein; western blot supports an ~8 kDa protein; AlphaFold2 prediction showed 'an alpha helix and a disordered sequence'.

Source_Species.reason: SWK is studied in Arabidopsis and described as originating in Arabidopsis thaliana.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper is on Arabidopsis thaliana and SWK is a de novo gene in this species.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The study states all Arabidopsis plants used were Col-0 ecotype.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional analyses were performed in Arabidopsis.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The experiments were conducted in Arabidopsis thaliana.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Mutant and OE analyses were done in the Col-0 background.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: The paper states SWK 'mainly localizes at the nucleus, but is also present in the cytoplasm (exclude chloroplasts) and the cell membrane'.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: RT-qPCR showed SWK is highly expressed in dry seed, imbibing seed, and silique.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The same Col-0 Arabidopsis material was used; the reported expression pattern is high in dry seed, imbibing seed, and silique.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Hydrogen peroxide was directly measured and linked to SWK. Folate was not directly measured for SWK; the paper only infers SWK involvement in the folate biosynthesis pathway via DHFS expression and prior literature on cytHPPK/DHPS affecting folate.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Hydrogen peroxide as a reactive oxygen-related metabolite is supported; classifying folate as a SWK terminal metabolite is indirect because folate itself was not measured in SWK lines.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Hydrogen peroxide and RNA-seq/qPCR analyses were performed in imbibing seeds under mannitol stress, supporting seed/imbibing seed as the site for the measured metabolite-related effects.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The role of lower hydrogen peroxide in supporting osmotic-stress germination is supported. The statement about folate supporting one-carbon metabolism is generally true biology but was not established as a SWK-specific functional output in this paper.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: SWK was tested under 400 mM mannitol osmotic stress and exogenous ABA stress.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are reported for SWK: T-DNA mutant, CRISPR/Cas9 knockouts, OE, RT-qPCR, western blot, localization, RNA-seq, H2O2 measurement, and yeast two-hybrid.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show SWK promotes seed germination under osmotic stress, mutant lines accumulate more H2O2, and SWK affects DHFS expression linking it to folate-biosynthesis-related regulation.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point is supported by the Results: SWK was the only de novo gene with clear phenotype; mutant/OE effects under 400 mM mannitol; ~8 kDa translated protein mainly nuclear; cytHPPK/DHPS upstream; GSTF9/H2O2 effects; DHFS reduction in SWK knockout.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper reports mutants had significantly higher germination than an OE line under exogenous ABA stress, implying increased ABA sensitivity with SWK/OE.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The Discussion suggests broader implications for drought-resistant crops and climate change, but direct breeding/engineering utility is not experimentally validated.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods explicitly state 35S::SWK-GFP and 35S::GFP were used for localization. The overexpression constructs used pCAMBIA2300 with C-terminal GFP, implying constitutive transgenic expression, but 35S is explicitly stated for localization constructs.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: The paper states none of the 3 upstream/downstream proteins directly interact with SWK by yeast two-hybrid; these include cytHPPK/DHPS, GSTF9, and DHFS.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper describes SWK as a de novo Arabidopsis gene from noncoding sequence integrated into stress/redox and folate-related pathways, and reports association of SWK presence with lower precipitation of the driest quarter in African accessions.

Omics_Data.reason: The paper reports eGWAS using leaf RNA-seq from 414 accessions and seed/imbibing-seed RNA-seq under control and 400 mM mannitol; GSA accession CRA014531 is given.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper names GSTF9 and cites literature on its function, but does not explicitly state in the Results that it is a phi-class glutathione S-transferase.

Source_Species.reason: GSTF9 is studied as an Arabidopsis gene in this Arabidopsis thaliana work.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The gene is discussed in Arabidopsis thaliana.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Comparisons were made in Col-0 background lines and mutants.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: SWK mutant, WT, and OE used are Col-0 background materials.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Under stress, OE had higher GSTF9 expression than WT, and WT higher than mutant.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same supported pattern in the experimental Arabidopsis Col-0-derived lines.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The paper links GSTF9 to hydrogen peroxide degradation and measures H2O2 differences in the relevant genotypes.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Hydrogen peroxide is a reactive oxygen-related metabolite, consistent with the paper's stress/redox context.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: GSTF9 expression and H2O2 measurements were made in imbibing seeds under mannitol stress.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper states GSTF9 increases resistance to mannitol stress by degrading hydrogen peroxide, connecting detoxification to stress resistance/germination.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: GSTF9 was analyzed under 400 mM mannitol osmotic stress.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: GSTF9 was identified from RNA-seq candidate analysis, validated by RT-qPCR, and interpreted alongside H2O2 measurements in genotypes affecting the pathway.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results support GSTF9 as downstream of SWK and cytHPPK/DHPS, with higher GSTF9 associated with lower H2O2 and improved stress germination in the pathway context.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All listed points match the Results: candidate downstream gene from RNA-seq, expression trend OE>WT>mutant, inverse association with H2O2, and similar reduced pattern in cytHPPK/DHPS mutant.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is an extrapolation; the paper does not experimentally test GSTF9 engineering or breeding application.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Yeast two-hybrid showed no direct interaction between SWK and the tested upstream/downstream proteins, including GSTF9.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper indeed cites prior work for GSTF9's role in mannitol resistance/H2O2 degradation and validates it here as a downstream node rather than the main discovery target.

Omics_Data.reason: GSTF9 was identified from imbibing-seed RNA-seq under control and 400 mM mannitol; CRA014531 is the study accession.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Materials list Salk_076763C (AT5G41480), corresponding to DHFS in this study context.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The Results refer to DHFS by name and as part of folate biosynthesis, but do not explicitly state the full family/domain label 'Dihydrofolate synthetase/folylpolyglutamate synthetase family'.

Source_Species.reason: DHFS is analyzed in Arabidopsis thaliana.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The study organism is Arabidopsis thaliana.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Mutant and WT materials are in Col-0 background.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The experiments were carried out in Arabidopsis thaliana.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper states DHFS expression in SWK-knockout lines was significantly lower than in WT.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same experimentally supported expression pattern in the tested Col-0-derived lines.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: DHFS is part of folate biosynthesis, but folate was not directly measured in this paper for DHFS-related experiments.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Folate is a vitamin, and DHFS is discussed within folate biosynthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The paper does not state folate accumulation specifically in seed for DHFS.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: General folate functions are not experimentally established for DHFS in this study.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: DHFS was discussed in the broader SWK/mannitol pathway context, but the specific RT-qPCR statement for DHFS reduction in SWK knockouts is not explicitly tied to 400 mM mannitol in the text.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: DHFS was evaluated via eGWAS candidate analysis, mutant/knockdown test for SWK expression, and RT-qPCR in SWK knockout versus WT.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The paper supports DHFS as a downstream folate-pathway gene influenced by SWK, with reduced expression in SWK knockouts.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: The Results state DHFS had the strongest eGWAS signal but its knockdown did not alter SWK expression, and DHFS expression was significantly lower in SWK knockouts.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: No breeding or engineering application for DHFS was tested or proposed directly in the Results.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: DHFS was among the tested upstream/downstream proteins that showed no direct interaction with SWK by yeast two-hybrid.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper states genes downstream of DHFS did not change, possibly due to feedback inhibition in folate metabolism.

Omics_Data.reason: DHFS is discussed in eGWAS using 414 accessions and RT-qPCR validation in SWK knockout lines.
